rejected_candidates.title: Single shared router account socializes liquidation risk across unrelated users
rejected_candidates.reason: Primarily an architectural pooling model observation; no standalone permissionless exploit was shown in `LayerZero/**` without relying on separate accounting bugs already tracked.

rejected_candidates.title: Cross-chain borrowIndex can be zero causing division revert
rejected_candidates.reason: Unsupported: `LToken.initialize` sets `borrowIndex` to `1e18`, so normal listed markets do not start at zero.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation close-factor uses stale principal instead of accrued debt
supply function uses pre-mint exchange rate for accounting
No access control on claimLend distribution function
rejected_candidates.reason: Not reportable as stated: `claimLend` transfers accrued tokens to listed holders, not to the caller; unauthorized callers cannot redirect rewards via this path.

rejected_candidates.title: Cross-chain liquidation sends amount instead of seizeTokens to collateral chain
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate root cause already captured (notably F-018; related execution-failure effects also covered elsewhere).

rejected_candidates.title: borrowWithInterestSame divides by zero when borrowIndex is zero
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: function guards `borrowIndex != 0` and returns zero otherwise; no direct division-by-zero path.

rejected_candidates.title: redeem uses stale exchange rate for transfer calculation
Cross-chain repay allows repaying more than borrowed due to index mismatch
rejected_candidates.reason: Insufficiently supported/incomplete candidate; provided path does not demonstrate bypass of `require(repayAmountFinal <= borrowedAmount)`.
